title: sample_registry sample_groups row
description: Schema for one row of sample_registry/sample_groups.tsv. Each row declares one named sample group. The group_id is the primary key and is referenced as a foreign key by results_registry manifest rows (who_1.group_id, who_2.group_id). Members of the group live in sample_registry/groups/<group_id>.txt.

group_id.description: Primary key. Convention: lowercase with underscores. Examples: 'all_226', 'unrelated_81', 'ancestry_K8_Q3', 'inv_LG12_17_HOM_REF'.

chrom.description: Optional: chromosome this group applies to (for inv_* carrier groups). '.' for cohort-wide groups.

inv_id.description: Optional: candidate_id this group applies to (for inv_* carrier groups). '.' for cohort-wide groups.

subgroup.description: Optional: subgroup tag. '.' for top-level groups.

dimension.description: Chat-16 addition. Optional metadata tag classifying which structural dimension this group represents. Values: '.' (default/top-level), 'karyotype' (top-level karyotype for a candidate: HOM_REF/HET/HOM_INV), 'karyotype_subcluster' (sub-cluster inside a karyotype, from recursive PCA), 'ancestry' (from NGSadmix/local Q), 'ghsl' (GHSL haplotype band), 'family' (pedigree family), 'intersect' (intersection of dimensions). The database does not interpret this field; classification scripts may filter on it. See DATABASE_DESIGN.md § Sample group naming convention.

members_file.description: Path to file containing one sample_id per line. Typically groups/<group_id>.txt relative to sample_registry/.

n.description: Number of samples in the group. Must match number of non-empty lines in members_file.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the group represents.

created.description: Group creation / last-overwrite timestamp (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS). Used as the 'group_version' when referenced by results_registry rows. A mismatch between this value and the stored group_version flags stale results.
